evals.prompt: I want to implement a dropzone upload component from this figma spec https://www.figma.com/design/rKUVGKb7Kw3d6YGoQl6Ho7/Flowbite-Component-Mapping?node-id=33308-25180&m=dev - it should go in libs/components and use existing components where relevant like bit-label
evals.expected_output: Creates dropzone component files (component.ts, component.html, index.ts, stories.ts), adds i18n keys, updates barrel exports, uses bit-label and bitButton, verifies in Storybook

evals.prompt: Build a progress bar component from this design https://www.figma.com/design/rKUVGKb7Kw3d6YGoQl6Ho7/Flowbite-Component-Mapping?node-id=33308-25400&m=dev - put it in libs/components/src/progress
evals.expected_output: Creates or updates progress component files following codebase conventions, maps Figma colors to semantic tokens, creates stories with multiple variants, uses I18nPipe for any text, verifies in Storybook

evals.prompt: Here's the Figma for a toast notification component https://www.figma.com/design/rKUVGKb7Kw3d6YGoQl6Ho7/Flowbite-Component-Mapping?node-id=33308-26000&m=dev - create it as an Angular component in the component library with stories
evals.expected_output: Explores existing toast component patterns, creates/updates component matching Figma, uses semantic design tokens, all strings localized via I18nPipe, stories cover success/error/warning/info variants, Storybook verification via Playwright

Infers component name from 'bit-rating-stars', fetches the Figma design, explores codebase conventions, creates component files with stories, uses I18nPipe for text, verifies in Storybook — all without needing explicit instructions beyond the name and link
